cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The question is asking what upset Melanie during the National Championships. The options are A to D. First, I need to refer back to the text. Let me scan through the relevant part. The story mentions Melanie entered her first triathlon at 14 and won the junior section. Then she entered the National Championships. The key part here is when she talks about her experience there. The text says: "I was following this man and suddenly we came to the sea. We realised then that we had gone wrong. I ended up cycling 20 kilometres too far. I cried all the way through the running." So, the problem here was that she and the man she was following made a mistake in the race course, which led her to cycle an extra 20 km. That would explain why she came nowhere in the competition. The mistake during the cycling part of the race is what upset her. Looking at the options, option C says "She made a mistake during part of the race." That matches with the information given. Option A is about being tricked by another competitor. The text doesn't mention any trickery; it's about a navigational error.
